Wanna customise your home to your liking? The HOA will fine you because they want the value of their property to go up, which means YOUR property needs to look as dull as possible.
it's always the people who are not in the Military.
OH YES!
If you don't know... http://www.ghpc.at/at/startseite/
fucker has a stand
The Telegraph is basically the Daily Mail for people who wear suits daily.
V I O L E N C E
again. http://www.ghpc.at/at/startseite/

item.title: Biang Biang Noodles
item.description: Biang Biang noodles. They're those super wide, hand-pulled noodles from Shaanxi province in China. **FP Edit: hey thank you everybody for getting me to the front page again :-) when I don't have time to put my own recipes on here, I like to put others. It's interesting some of the comments you guys made and so far as preferences and thank you to the kind soul who posted the actual recipe from the IG account where this came from :-)
item.dc:creator: PlantBasedChef

item.title: The Igudar, located in Agadir, Morocco, represent one of the oldest known banking systems in the world. These structures, also known as "agadir" or "igoudar" (plural of "agadir"), are ancient communal granaries used by the Amazigh (Berber) people. Via His

item.title: There's always tomorrow....?
item.description: Me and my wife were together for over 20 years. She died in my arms 3 years ago, and I went the way of the downward spiral of alchohol. I've pulled myself together with the help of amazing friends and family. But it's getting harder and harder every day, not being able to hold someone. Thx for going with me on this pitty trip, I live you all, Imgur!!!

item.title: Got to sleep in this morning, it was awesome
item.description: For a year now, my husband and I have lived separately. He got a job in DC, I have a great job in the Midwest, but can't do it remote. Nor can we live in DC on a single income. We have two kids under 3yrs old, and when he moved out east, our little man was only 5mo old. Since then, the husband comes home 1 week per month, but this stretch was more like 6weeks since he's been home. I normally wake up at 4:30am, kids get up at 5:30, and out the door by 6:15am. But today, the husband's home, and took charge. Without me even asking. He even did the dishes from last night before I got out of bed. He got the kids up, took them downstairs, got them breakfast, and let me sleep in until 7am!!! What a treat!
item.dc:creator: twatburglar
